_interpretation.cube_recipe: THE RESULT: Opus-authored METHOD (DFA/step-table) poured into the Cube 3.0 substrate and executed by the recall-loop = 100% on unseen inputs, all 4 skills, from 4-21 rules. Capability the plain Slate cannot give.
_interpretation.tiny_bare: llama3.2:1b alone: divisibility at chance (~50% on balanced sets), 4-digit addition 0%. The 1B lacks these skills.
_interpretation.flashcard_cube: Memorising answers (Opus/true labels) does NOT transfer: chance on div, 15% (BELOW chance) on parity. Proves it's the method, not the examples.
_interpretation.tiny_cube_e2e: CAVEAT — this is NOT a cube measurement. It measures the 1B's reliability as a TOOL-CALLER: it hands the cube the wrong operand (~half the time it echoes the divisor, not the dividend). The cube computes 100% on whatever operand it gets. Fix = constrained/JSON decoding or a stronger front-end model, not a better cube.
